title: Finance Section: Separate Category Reports with PDF/Excel Export and Editable Entries

requirements.text: Add a dedicated report section for each expense category in the Finance page. Each category (e.g., Rent, Salaries, Supplies, Equipment, Utilities, Miscellaneous, etc.) must display its own filtered list of expense entries with subtotals, filterable by date range.

quotes: make all expenses report separately for total Finance with each category report generation

acceptanceCriteria: Each expense category renders in its own report section or tab
Each category section shows all entries belonging to that category
Each category section shows a subtotal sum for filtered entries
Date range filter applies per category report

requirements.text: Add a Total Finance Summary Report section that aggregates all expense categories, showing per-category totals and an overall grand total. This summary must be accessible from the Finance page and include a date range filter.

quotes: total Finance with each category report generation

acceptanceCriteria: A Total Finance Summary section is visible on the Finance page
Summary shows each category name alongside its total amount
Summary shows a grand total across all categories
Summary respects the selected date range filter

requirements.text: Add PDF export functionality to each individual category report and to the Total Finance Summary. When the user clicks 'Export PDF' for a report, a print-ready PDF is generated and downloaded containing the report header, date range, category entries (or summary rows), and totals.
acceptanceCriteria: Each category report has an 'Export PDF' button
Total Finance Summary has an 'Export PDF' button
Clicking 'Export PDF' downloads a formatted PDF of that report
PDF includes report title, date range, line items, and totals

requirements.text: Add Excel (.xlsx) export functionality to each individual category report and to the Total Finance Summary. When the user clicks 'Export Excel', a properly formatted .xlsx file is downloaded with columns for date, description, category, and amount.
acceptanceCriteria: Each category report has an 'Export Excel' button
Total Finance Summary has an 'Export Excel' button
Clicking 'Export Excel' downloads a .xlsx file
Excel file contains date, description, category, and amount columns plus a totals row

requirements.text: Make all expense entries in every category report editable at any time. Each row in a category report must have an edit button that opens an inline or modal form allowing the user to update the amount, date, description, and category. Changes must be persisted to the backend.
acceptanceCriteria: Every expense entry row in each category report has an edit action
Clicking edit opens a form pre-filled with the entry's current values
User can update amount, date, description, and category
Saved changes are persisted to the backend and reflected immediately in the report

requirements.text: Add a print-optimized layout for each category report and the Total Finance Summary. When the user clicks 'Print', the browser print dialog opens showing a clean, formatted view with report title, date range, entries table, and totals — with navigation and action buttons hidden.
acceptanceCriteria: Each category report and the Total Finance Summary have a 'Print' button
Print layout hides navigation, sidebars, and action buttons
Print view shows report title, date range, all entries, and totals
Print layout is clean and legible on standard paper sizes

constraints: All Motoko logic must remain in the single backend/main.mo actor file
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
Expense edit operations must use the existing backend mutation patterns established in useQueries.ts
nonGoals: Adding new expense categories beyond those already supported by the backend
Changing the overall Finance page navigation structure
Real-time collaborative editing of expense entries
